meta.next_session: 2026-06-27 6:00 AM PDT (Daily Brief — market closed Sat; deposit-routing executes Mon 2026-06-29)

thoughts.label: MRVL STOP-LOSS EXECUTED
thoughts.text: MRVL hit its -5% trigger ($265.08) intraday and was sold via agentic market order at $263.11 (12:04 PDT) — realized -5.71%. First completed stop-loss cycle. The decline was a broad semiconductor sector rotation, not a company-specific catalyst, so the custom-AI-ASIC thesis stays intact: redeploy to SOXX per the §13.3 migration plan rather than abandon the theme.

thoughts.label: NVDA STILL IN STOP-ZONE
thoughts.text: NVDA closed $191.72 / AH $192.41 — only 1.25% above its $190.04 manual stop, -3.81% from the $200.04 entry. Z=-1.84 and BB%B=0.04 show it pinned to the lower band; MACD Bearish. Highest-priority manual check Monday open. A gap-down breaches -5%.

thoughts.label: ROUTE DEPOSIT TO ETF TIER
thoughts.text: A $100 deposit landed (cash now $111.37 incl. $11.37 MRVL proceeds). Per §17 deposit-routing, capital goes to the most underweight tier: ETF is 8.6% vs the 50% Accumulation target. Lead candidate SOXX (MRVL replacement, -5.69% dip today) and/or QQQ (composite +0.173, ARKK migration target). Execute Monday — no weekend orders.

thoughts.label: SEMI → SOFTWARE ROTATION
thoughts.text: Clear one-day rotation: semiconductors sold off hard (MU -7.5%, SOXX -5.7%, AVGO -4.0%, ARM -3.7%, NVDA -2.1%) while software/security/AI-application names rallied (SNOW +9.4%, MSFT +5.3%, NET +4.5%, PANW +3.6%, CRWD +3.2%). The portfolio is semi-heavy and felt it — reinforcing the §13 push toward ETF diversification.

thoughts.label: FIRST ACCURACY GRADE (FRIDAY)
thoughts.text: Weekly check: MRVL graded -1.0 (early stop-out). Win rate 0% on 1 of 4 evaluated; INTC/ARKK/NVDA remain pending their 5-day windows (07-01/07-02). No new DO_NOT_TRADE condition — the loss was a pre-engine baseline with no signal conditions, and -1.0 is not below the -1.5 threshold.

thoughts.label: COMPOUND GATE CORRECTED PANW
thoughts.text: The 6 AM thesis-only promotion of PANW to Predicted Victory was reversed at the first live engine run: compound gate 1/5 (RSI 70.5 Overbought, Z=1.87, Score -0.062, MACD Bearish). PANW held in Pipeline. CRWD passed 4/5 and was promoted. The quant gate is doing its job — blocking a momentum chase on thesis alone.

learned_patterns.pattern: First completed stop-loss cycle (MRVL): entry $279.03 → stop-out $263.11 (-5.71%) within ~1 trading day. The decline was a broad semi sector rotation, not a company catalyst — so §7.1 routes to 'thesis intact, technical decline' → redeploy to SOXX rather than abandon the theme.

Semis → software rotation on 2026-06-26: semiconductors -2% to -7.5% (MU, SOXX, AVGO, ARM, AMD, NVDA) while software/security rallied +3% to +9% (SNOW, MSFT, NET, PANW, CRWD, RXRX). A semi-heavy book underperforms in this rotation — reinforces §13 ETF diversification.

The compound entry gate caught an overbought false-promote: PANW's 6 AM thesis-only PV promotion was reversed at 1/5 (RSI 70.5, Z 1.87, MACD Bearish). Thesis alone is not sufficient — the quant gate blocks momentum chasing.
MU's +11.78% post-earnings spike fully reversed (-7.46% next session) — validating the prior Z>2.0 entry block that prevented buying the top. Statistically overbought spikes mean-revert.

ALB Z=-2.18 (OVERSOLD) but Trend50=Down: an oversold quant flag is NOT a buy when the 50-day trend is broken (fails Gate 1, structural not technical). Mean-reversion signals require an intact uptrend.

New deposits route to the most underweight tier (§17), not to a signal-based single-stock entry. ETF tier at 8.6% vs 50% target → deposit goes to ETF; a SOXX dip (-5.69%) makes the redeploy doubly attractive.

top_opportunities.thesis: iShares Semiconductor ETF — the designated MRVL replacement under the §13.3 migration plan. It captures NVDA/AMD/AVGO/TSM/MRVL/INTC with diversification, removing single-name execution risk. Today's -5.69% sector-selloff dip is a discounted entry, and the ETF tier is the most underweight (8.6% vs the 50% Accumulation target) so deposit-routing points here too. Cleaner signals than single semis (§13.5).
